title: Ready to Roll
blocks: Today marked a monumental shift: I finally got a bicycle. True, it was
worn and needed repair, but it was mine. With a new front tire, pedals,
rearview mirror, rear seat, and other essentials, I was almost ready for
the road. The total cost stung---a whole 150 pesos---but it was a
My preparations didn't stop there. I purchased a Spanish-English
dictionary, a small but significant tool for the upcoming encounters.
And tomorrow, I have plans to buy a map of South America and some shorts

While Buenos Aires has grown homey, I mustn't forget the sense of momentum and continuity: I reminded myself of the emotions I felt the day I lost my backpack---utter perplexity and forlornness, followed by a strange clarity about the path ahead. I needed to leave before the line between comfort and complacency blurred, making my next step a hesitant one.
